public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize the eGTM-based 3-phase inverter PWM. Steps: 1) Enable the eGTM module and enable required clocks for the ATOM time base and DTM dead-time generation (FXCLK_0 for ATOM and CLK0 for DTM). 2) Initialize a unified PWM configuration with defaults, select ATOM submodule in the chosen cluster, and set center-aligned mode with a 20 kHz base frequency. 3) For three channels (base + 2 synchronous), set initial phase = 0, independent duty initialization, and attach DTM dead-time of 1.0 microsecond for both rising and falling edges on each channel to produce complementary high-/low-side outputs. 4) Assign output pin mappings for each channel’s primary and complementary outputs using the generic pin-map macros (driver handles muxing and pad settings). 5) Configure a period-event interrupt on the base channel: route it to CPU0 with the specified priority, and leave interrupt pointers for the remaining sync channels as NULL. 6) Install the ISR handler in the CPU interrupt system. 7) Initialize the PWM driver with the populated config and channels, update internal AGC/TGC references, and start all configured channels synchronously to guarantee a common time base (syncStart). 8) Enable synchronous update behavior (handled through configuration and unified driver) so that duty updates across channels maintain coherent timing. 9) Configure the LED GPIO as a push-pull output and drive it to a known initial state. The ISR will toggle this LED at each PWM period event.

Perform a ramp update of the three PWM channel duties with wrap-around. For each of the three channels: if current duty + STEP reaches or exceeds 100%, reset that channel's duty to 0% before applying the increment. Then add STEP to each channel’s duty. After updating the three duty values in the local duty array, apply all new duties immediately using the unified driver’s multi-channel immediate update API so all channels update coherently in the same PWM cycle (syncUpdate honored).
